SpaNorm 1.7.6

Determine the accelerator memory budget for blocked fitting

Source: R/gpuFunctions.R
getGPUMemoryBudget.Rd

Resolves (once per session, then cached – see resetGPUCache) the accelerator memory budget (bytes) to use for memory-aware gene-blocked fitting: free CUDA memory via nvidia-smi, a conservative fraction of total system memory for MPS (unified memory has no per-process query), or a fixed fallback if detection fails. A user-supplied gpu.mem.budget bypasses detection entirely – set it to Inf to disable blocking outright. Returns Inf when no accelerator is in use. Exposed so downstream packages (e.g. spiDE) doing their own GPU-memory-aware blocking share the same budget/cache as SpaNorm's own fitting.

Usage

getGPUMemoryBudget(gpu.mem.budget = NULL)

Arguments

gpu.mem.budget

NULL (default, auto-detect) or a single positive number (bytes).

Value

a numeric, the memory budget in bytes (Inf if no accelerator is in use).

Examples

getGPUMemoryBudget()
#> [1] Inf

On this page
